actual_meaning: yeah right / no way (sarcastic dismissal)
note: Sarcastic; literal 'bell' is nonsense. Tamil/Hindi both need an idiomatic sarcasm marker, not translation.

actual_meaning: nonsense / I don't believe a word of that (contemptuous dismissal)
note: Indian-English fixed expression; literal foot makes no sense. Hindi often keeps it as-is; Tamil dub needs a scoff word like 'cheh' to land.

actual_meaning: for no reason / just because / nothing in particular
note: Deflecting filler dismissal. Tamil 'summa' is the perfect equivalent; literal Hindi gloss loses the shrug tone.

literal: let it remain / let it be
actual_meaning: forget it / drop it / never mind
note: Hindi-native, dub-as-is. Tamil 'irukkattum / vidu' carries it; tone of resigned dismissal must be matched.

actual_meaning: oh come on / quit it (playful pleading dismissal)
note: Tone-dependent particle 'na'. Tamil 'vaa da / poda' shifts by context; literal 'walk' wrong.

actual_meaning: get lost / buzz off (dismissive brush-off)
note: Brusque dismissal. Tamil 'po da' fits; literal 'walk-move' reads as a stage direction, not contempt.

literal: become side / go to the side
actual_meaning: get out of my way / move aside
note: Hinglish (English 'side' + Hindi). Tamil 'oranga po / vilagu'; literal is comprehensible but the curt slang register needs adapting.

actual_meaning: don't create a fuss / stop making a spectacle
note: English 'scene' as loan. Tamil 'scene podaadhe' actually exists; Hindi keeps it. Literal 'don't do scene' opaque to non-Indian dub.

actual_meaning: stop being theatrical / quit the melodrama
note: 'Drama' loanword. Tamil 'drama pannaadhe' is idiomatic; needs the loan kept, not a native 'natakam' which sounds literary.

actual_meaning: a drama queen / over-dramatic person or act
note: Culture-bound to a North Indian theatre genre. No Tamil equivalent; dub needs 'drama' or 'over-acting' rewrite entirely.

actual_meaning: don't pick a fight / don't mess with (someone)
note: 'Panga' is Punjabi-origin slang with no clean gloss. Tamil 'modhaadhe / lavadai vendaam'; literal impossible.

actual_meaning: a fight, beef, or risky mess you get into
note: Standalone noun; Punjabi/Hindi slang. Tamil has no single word — 'gandagol / pirachanai'. Register hard to keep.

actual_meaning: a mess, dispute, or shady complication
note: Mumbai slang. Tamil 'gandagol' approximates; the street-shady connotation is lost in a neutral translation.

actual_meaning: teasing / harassment (often of women); messing with someone
note: Reduplicative; ranges playful to euphemism for harassment. Tamil 'kindal / tொந்தரவு' splits the senses — context decides; risky to dub flat.

actual_meaning: chaos / messy disorder / a clusterf***
note: Delhi slang, vulgar-tinged. No literal gloss, no Tamil equivalent — full rewrite to 'gandagol / kuழப்பம்' loses the edge.

actual_meaning: a jobless time-waster / aimless loafer
note: North Indian slang. Tamil 'sumaa irukkaravan / vetti' close; literal 'empty' misses the mocking person-label.

actual_meaning: a waste of time / pointless activity
note: Indian-English compress of 'waste of time'. Tamil 'time waste' is used verbatim; Hindi too. Easy but the clipped grammar is the flavour.

actual_meaning: we're done for / it's ruined (situation went bad)
note: Idiomatic doom. Literal 'cut' nonsense. Tamil needs 'mudinju pochu' rewrite; tone of fatalism essential.

actual_meaning: we're screwed / we're in for it now
note: Set phrase of impending doom. Literal verb meaningless alone. Tamil 'maatikittom'; full idiom swap needed.

actual_meaning: he's finished / done for / a goner
note: Idiom for being ruined/dead. Literal 'gone from work' misleads. Tamil 'gtti pochu / poyiteer'; rewrite required.

actual_meaning: badly screwed / in deep trouble
note: Marathi-origin ('waat laagli'). Literal opaque even in Hindi. Tamil 'vootu poச்chu / matti'; pure idiom, full rewrite.

actual_meaning: got thrashed / utterly defeated / done for
note: Sarcastic 'a band plays for the loser'. Literal celebratory image inverts the meaning. Tamil needs total rewrite to 'mudinju pochu'.

actual_meaning: thrashed / destroyed / wrecked someone
note: From 'band bajana'. Causative; literal 'made it play' meaningless. Tamil 'adichu thoolu'; full idiom swap.

actual_meaning: it's finished / no chance left now
note: English gaming loan used as doom-marker. Tamil/Hindi both say 'game over' verbatim; near-universal, low difficulty.

literal: had to give instead of take
actual_meaning: it backfired / ended up worse off than before
note: Proverbial reversal idiom. Literal gloss confusing. Tamil 'labam thedi nashtam vந்thuchu'; needs idiomatic reconstruction.

actual_meaning: too clever for one's own good / a smart-aleck
note: Indian-English coinage. Tamil 'over smart' borrowed directly; native 'romba thimiru' shifts nuance. Keep the loan.

actual_meaning: showing off / overacting / putting on airs
note: South-India-heavy Indian-English. Tamil 'over action' is native slang; Hindi too. Don't translate, transcreate the loan.

actual_meaning: is overdoing it / taking it too far
note: 'Over' as verb-loan. Tamil 'over panறan'; literal makes no sense in any target. Keep loanword.

actual_meaning: acting cocky / throwing around an ego
note: 'Attitude' loanword. Tamil 'attitude kaattறan' verbatim. Native words ('ahankaram') sound formal; keep loan for register.

actual_meaning: to act high and mighty / play hard to get / not give attention
note: Idiom from market 'bhav' (rate). Literal 'eat price' nonsense. Tamil 'edam kudukala / thimiru kaattறan'; full rewrite.

actual_meaning: don't pay them attention / don't entertain them
note: Same 'bhav' market metaphor. Literal opaque. Tamil 'edam kudukaadhe'; idiomatic equivalent exists but not a literal one.

actual_meaning: to do something effortlessly / in a snap
note: Gesture-based; Tamil 'chutti pottu' / finger-snap idiom exists. Mostly translatable, image survives across cultures.

actual_meaning: to fool / dupe / take someone for a ride
note: Owl = fool in Hindi culture; in Tamil owl isn't 'fool'. Literal 'make owl' confusing — Tamil 'emaathறadhu' needed.

actual_meaning: to make a scapegoat / set someone up to be fooled
note: Goat = sacrificial dupe. Tamil 'aattai aakறadhu' not idiomatic; needs 'maatti vidறadhu' rewrite. Animal metaphor doesn't port.

actual_meaning: a coward / chicken / scaredy-cat
note: Crude Hindi slang (root is vulgar 'fart with fear'). Tamil 'kozhandhai dhairiyam / bayandhaangoli'; no literal gloss, register hard.

actual_meaning: a coward / timid person
note: Transparent Hindi word. Tamil 'kோழை / bayandhaangoli' direct. Translatable, just match the mocking tone.

actual_meaning: a coward (Indian-English, often as a taunt 'chicken!')
note: English borrowing of US 'chicken=coward'. Tamil/Hindi viewers get it via films; native bird word ('kozhi') not a coward-insult, so keep loan.

actual_meaning: got deflated / lost all bravado / nerve broke
note: Deflation metaphor. Tamil 'kaathu pochu' actually works as slang; Hindi native. Light adaptation, image mostly survives.

actual_meaning: reputation reduced to mush / total public humiliation
note: Dessert metaphor (mushy mixed falooda). Culture-bound; Tamil has no falooda idiom. Full rewrite to 'maanam poச்chu'.

actual_meaning: insult / public humiliation / loss of face
note: Urdu/Hindi; Tamil 'avamaanam' is a clean equivalent. Translatable; only register casualness to keep.

actual_meaning: public humiliation / disrespect (used as Hinglish verb too)
note: English loan used in 'insult ho gaya'. Tamil 'avamaanam'; the loan-as-verb construction is the Indian flavour to preserve.

actual_meaning: a thrashing / beating (literal or figurative drubbing)
note: Hindi slang noun. Tamil 'adi / odhachu' close; the casual 'got a thukai' register hard to keep literal.

actual_meaning: a thorough thrashing / getting beaten up
note: 'Laundry' metaphor for a beating. Literal 'washing' wrong. Tamil 'nalla adi'; idiom doesn't port, needs rewrite.

actual_meaning: a severe beating / getting pulverised
note: Hindi slang. Tamil 'thallu / adi'; the crushing intensity is idiomatic, literal grinding image not used.

actual_meaning: come off it, you're lying / I don't buy it
note: Mostly literal but 'chal' as a dismissive opener is the slang. Tamil 'poda poi solra'; tone over words.

actual_meaning: forget about it / let it go (casual)
note: Hindi-native. Tamil 'vidu da'; the relaxed 'na' particle is the texture, easy to dub.

actual_meaning: let it be / drop it
note: Standard Hindi. Tamil 'poga vidu / vidu'; clean equivalent, minor tone-matching.

actual_meaning: that's enough / cut it out
note: Hindi 'bas' = enough. Tamil 'pothum da'; directly translatable, register casual.

actual_meaning: shut up / be quiet (curt)
note: Hindi-native. Tamil 'vaaya moodu / summa iru'; translatable, intensity scales with delivery.

actual_meaning: buzz off / get lost (sharp brush-off)
note: One-word dismissal. Tamil 'po da'; literal 'move' too neutral, needs the contemptuous clip.

actual_meaning: a shady issue / something fishy / a mess
note: Mumbai slang for fishiness. Literal 'sag' meaningless. Tamil 'edhuvo gandagol'; full rewrite, no equivalent.

actual_meaning: a fixed arrangement / shady deal / unofficial fix
note: English loan with Indian shady-deal sense. Tamil 'setting' used too; native words lose the wink. Keep loan.

actual_meaning: a glitch / problem / hitch
note: Bambaiya slang. Tamil 'pirachanai / gandagol'; the casual 'kuch locha hai' vibe doesn't port literally.

actual_meaning: things got messed up / a situation spiralled out of control
note: Food metaphor (spilled raita). Culture-bound; Tamil has no raita idiom. Rewrite to 'gandagol aayiruchu' loses colour.

actual_meaning: got into deep trouble (Marathi-Hindi)
note: Marathi import. Opaque outside Maharashtra. Tamil 'mati pochu'; pure idiom needing full reconstruction.

actual_meaning: so what will you do about it? / you can't do anything (taunt)
note: Challenge idiom; 'uproot' = achieve nothing. Tamil 'enna pудunga порe'; idiom porting hard, full transcreation.

actual_meaning: who do you think you are? / you're beneath this (contempt)
note: 'Aukaat' = social standing, Urdu-origin. Tamil 'un layakku enna'; the loaded class-contempt nuance is hard to dub neutrally.

actual_meaning: put someone in their place / exposed their true low worth
note: Same 'aukaat' metaphor. Tamil 'layakku kaatti'; the dismissive class jab needs careful tone, not literal.

actual_meaning: is ignoring me / not giving me any importance
note: Market 'bhaav' idiom. Literal nonsensical. Tamil 'edam kudukala / mதikkala'; idiomatic only, no literal route.

actual_meaning: forget it, you're not worth it (dismissive, mildly rude)
note: Curt rude dismissal. Tamil 'po da unna'; register is the challenge — keep it casual-rude, not vulgar in dub.

actual_meaning: how does it affect you? / mind your own business
note: Idiom of 'none of your loss'. Tamil 'unakku enna nashtam'; portable but the snappy rhythm needs adaptation.

actual_meaning: get lost / scram (slangy)
note: 'Phut' = scram in slang. Tamil 'po da / kalambu'; literal 'burst' wrong, need the disappear sense.

actual_meaning: get out / leave (curt, viral meme tone)
note: Meme-fueled ('nikal pehli fursat mein'). Tamil 'kalambu da'; literal 'exit' too plain for the dismissive bite.

actual_meaning: something's fishy / there's foul play
note: Proverb. Food metaphor; Tamil has its own 'edho fishy' phrasing but not this image. Rewrite, not translate.

actual_meaning: it's over / the end (often dramatic/threatening)
note: Hindi-native. Tamil 'velai mudinju / game over'; image of game-ending survives, low difficulty.

actual_meaning: goodbye / you're finished (mocking farewell)
note: Indian-English mocking farewell. Tamil borrows 'tata bye bye' verbatim from films; the sarcasm is in delivery.

actual_meaning: get out of here / scram / I don't believe you
note: Doubles as disbelief and dismissal. Tamil 'odu da / po inthikinde'; context splits sense, watch the dub line.

actual_meaning: stop the drama / cut the theatrics
note: 'Nautanki' culture-bound. Tamil 'drama nirthu'; the specific theatre-genre image must be generalised to 'drama'.

actual_meaning: stop pretending / quit the act
note: 'Natak' = play. Tamil 'natakam pannaadhe' is an exact cognate — Sanskrit-origin shared word. Rare easy case.

actual_meaning: flaunting his ego / being pompous
note: 'Ego' loanword. Tamil 'ego kaattறan'; native 'ahankaram' is formal, keep the casual loan.

actual_meaning: arrogance / haughty rigidity
note: Hindi 'stiffness=pride' metaphor. Tamil 'thimiru' is a strong match; literal 'stiffness' wouldn't read as pride.

actual_meaning: humbled them / knocked the arrogance out of them
note: Idiom built on 'akad'. Tamil 'thimiru adakki'; literal 'removed stiffness' opaque, needs idiom swap.

actual_meaning: got cocky / went hyper-aggressive suddenly
note: Bambaiya slang. Literal 'shone' totally wrong. Tamil 'mass aayitan / theri vந்தuchu'; full rewrite.

actual_meaning: for free / freeloading (and worthless by extension)
note: Slang variant of 'fokat'. Tamil 'free-ya / vetti'; the freeloader-contempt nuance needs care.

actual_meaning: casual time-killing / not serious (also dismissive of a person/thing)
note: Iconic Indian-English compound. Tamil 'timepass' verbatim. Translating to native loses the cultural shorthand.

actual_meaning: useless / worthless / pointless
note: Hindi slang. Tamil 'vetti / waste'; literal 'surplus' misses the dismissive 'useless'. Light adaptation.

actual_meaning: useless / good-for-nothing / rubbish
note: Transparent Hindi. Tamil 'vேst / panathukku aagaadhu'; translatable, casual register to keep.

actual_meaning: made a complete fool of someone / conned them
note: Vulgar Hindi root. Tamil 'mokka aakki / emaathti'; can't dub literally, must soften for broadcast register.

actual_meaning: cheated / conned / ripped someone off
note: Idiom ('chuna'=slaked lime). Literal opaque. Tamil 'emaathi vittan / mottai adichan'; full idiom reconstruction.

actual_meaning: fooled / cheated / pulled a fast one
note: 'Cap' = con metaphor. Literal hat image wrong. Tamil 'topi pottan' actually exists in Madras slang — rare partial match.

actual_meaning: deceived / tricked someone slyly
note: Slang, opaque even regionally. Tamil 'maatti vittan'; literal meaningless, full rewrite needed.

actual_meaning: to create hype / build a false buzz
note: 'Air' = hype metaphor. Tamil 'hype panறadhu / kaathu kalakறadhu'; image partly ports, but needs idiomatic handling.

actual_meaning: a damp squib / all hype, no result (fizzled out)
note: Sound-symbolic dismissal. Tamil 'phuss-nu pochu' borrowed; onomatopoeia mostly travels but spelling/sound must match dub.

actual_meaning: nothing / zilch / 'you'll get nothing' (with thumb gesture)
note: Tied to a rude thumb gesture. Tamil 'ஒன்னும் illa + gesture'; literal 'thumb' meaningless without the visual, dub-tricky.

actual_meaning: an itch to do something needlessly / unnecessary craving for trouble
note: Slang ('itch for drama'). Tamil 'arippu' is a literal+slang match interestingly; the playful tone needs preserving.

actual_meaning: a mischievous urge / itch to stir trouble ('keeda kaat raha')
note: 'A worm bit you' = restless urge. Tamil 'puழu kadichudha'; the bug-metaphor for mischief partly ports.

actual_meaning: stop nagging / quit annoying me
note: 'Eat brain' = annoy. Tamil 'thala saப்piduthe / mண்டaiya saப்pidaadhe' actually mirrors it — shared metaphor, light adaptation.

actual_meaning: oh just drop it, man
note: Casual rude dismissal. Tamil 'vidu da'; the 'yaar' camaraderie particle is the texture to keep.

actual_meaning: what rubbish / utter nonsense
note: 'Bakwaas' = nonsense. Tamil 'enna kuppa / mokka'; translatable, the exasperation is in delivery.

actual_meaning: shut up with the rubbish / cut the crap
note: Tamil 'mokka pேsaadhe / kuppa vேndaam'; clean equivalent, casual-curt tone to match.

actual_meaning: pfft / as if (dismissive scoff)
note: Pure interjection of contempt. Tamil 'cheh / che'; non-lexical, dub via the equivalent scoff sound, never translate.

actual_meaning: hmph / yeah right (skeptical dismissal)
note: Interjection. Tamil 'hmph / hunh'; ports as a grunt but the skeptical colour depends on voice acting.

actual_meaning: stop making excuses / quit the cover story
note: Tamil 'sakku சொல்லாதே'; translatable, 'chal' dismissive opener is the only slang texture.

actual_meaning: overused / stale / a tired old thing
note: 'Worn' = cliché metaphor. Tamil 'pழasaana / over-ya aana'; image partly ports, casual dismissive tone to keep.

actual_meaning: junk / trash / worthless stuff
note: Hindi/Urdu for scrap. Tamil 'kuppai / vேst'; translatable, the contemptuous use of it is the flavour.

actual_meaning: a dimwit / idiot
note: 'Lid' = fool slang. Literal nonsense as insult. Tamil 'mokka / loosu'; no equivalent object-metaphor, full rewrite.

actual_meaning: a good-for-nothing / lazy useless person
note: Hindi-native. Tamil 'sோmbேri / panathukku aagaadha aal'; translatable, mocking register to match.

actual_meaning: get out of here right now (curt eviction)
note: Compound dismissal. Tamil 'kalambu da inthikinde'; literal works but the curt menace needs delivery, not words.

actual_meaning: I'm fed up with you / done dealing with you
note: Tamil 'unnoda sலிச்சு போச்சு'; translatable, the exasperated dismissal tone is what carries it.

actual_meaning: got humiliated / situation went terribly downhill
note: Crude Hindi slang, opaque literal. Tamil 'mati pochu / kevalama pochu'; no equivalent, full rewrite + register care.

actual_meaning: got publicly embarrassed / lost face
note: Punjabi-tinged slang ('phajeeti'). Tamil 'avamaanam aayiruchu'; the casual mocking register has no literal route.

actual_meaning: worthless / cheap / of no value
note: 'Cowrie' = old currency idiom. Tamil 'kasukku aagaadha'; the antique-coin image needs rewrite, not literal.

actual_meaning: know your place / stay in your lane (threat)
note: 'Auqaat' Urdu class-term. Tamil 'un edathule iru'; the loaded class-contempt is hard to neutralise for dub.

literal: leave it friend, you won't understand
actual_meaning: forget it, this is over your head (dismissive)
note: Tamil 'vidu da, unakku puriyaadhu'; translatable, the condescending-dismissive blend is in tone.

actual_meaning: don't kick up a ruckus / stop creating mayhem
note: 'Bhasad' vulgar-slang for chaos. Tamil 'gandagol pannaadhe'; the crude register has no literal equivalent.
